request-ts.description: Timestamp for when the approval request was generated.
request-ts.x-typeDescription:
A date and time, expressed in the format described in RFC3339.
That is to say:

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ+-hh:mm

where YYYY is the year, MM is the month expressed as a two-digit
month (zero padding if required), DD is the day of the month,
expressed as a two digit value. T is the literal character 'T',
HH is the hour of the day expressed as a two digit number, using
the 24-hour clock, MM is the minute of the hour expressed as a
two digit number. Z is the literal character 'Z', followed by a
timezone offset expressed in hours (hh) and minutes (mm), both
expressed as two digit numbers. The time offset is specified as
a positive or negative offset to UTC using the '+' or '-'
character preceding the offset.

Optionally, fractional seconds can be expressed after the minute
of the hour as a decimal number of unspecified precision
reflecting fractions of a second.

password-auto-expire.description:
password-auto-expire is an automatic expiration interval (ISO 8601 duration)
that is applied whenever an owned MPSK is created or the password is reset.

An active Central NAC subscription is required to set this field.

This field applies to end-user provisioned MPSK only. It does not apply to
administrator managed MPSK or operations.

password-minimum-age.description:
password-minimum-age is a password minimum age (ISO 8601 duration) that limits
how often an end user can reset a password.

password-minimum-age duration must be at least one day less than the
password-auto-expire duration, in the case that both are set.

An active Central NAC subscription is required to set this field.

This field applies to end-user provisioned MPSK only. It does not apply to
administrator managed MPSK or operations.

ArubaCdaAuthProfile_WiredProfileConfig.description: Configuration for an authentication profile when supporting a wired network.

wired-profile.description:
Configuration for an authentication profile when supporting a wired network.

The wired field must be true, and the auth-type must be CAPTIVE_PORTAL, to configure a wired-profile.

items.description:
The names of the Access Point (AP) port profiles with which this authentication profile will used.

A port profile defines parameters on the port such as VLAN settings, authentication methods, link speed, PoE settings, and more.

For a CAPTIVE_PORTAL authentication profile, only wired interfaces with one of these port profiles will be configured with the captive portal.

At least one port profile must be specified.

At most 100 port profiles may be specified.

local-port-profiles.description: Local port profiles used for authentication profile.

name.description: Name of the local port profile.

scope-id.description: Scope Id for the local port profile.

x-enumDescriptions.OP_BEGINS_WITH:
BEGINS_WITH condition for use in Cloud Auth policy rules.
Example: 'Hello' BEGINS_WITH 'Hel'
x-enumDescriptions.OP_BEGINS_WITH_IGNORE_CASE:
String has prefix with case ignored.
Example: 'Hello' BEGINS_WITH_IGNORE_CASE 'heL'
x-enumDescriptions.OP_BELONGS_TO:
String present in a List.
Example: 'v1' BELONGS_TO ['v1', 'v2']
x-enumDescriptions.OP_CONTAINS:
Condition: String contains a substring.
Example: 'Hello' CONTAINS 'ell'.

List contains all elements of another list.
Example: ['a', 'b'] CONTAINS_ALL ['a', 'b']
x-enumDescriptions.OP_CONTAINS_ANY:
Condition: List contains a sublist.
Example: ['a', 'b', 'c'] CONTAINS_ANY ['z', 'c']
x-enumDescriptions.OP_CONTAINS_ELEM:
Condition: List contains a string.
Example: ['hello', 'world'] CONTAINS_ELEM 'world'
x-enumDescriptions.OP_CONTAINS_NONE:
List contains none of the elements of another list
Example: ['a', 'b'] CONTAINS_NONE ['a', 'd']

Example: 'Hello' ENDS_WITH 'llo'

network-login-host-override.description:
Override the expected host that the Central NAC captive portal should redirect back
to after it has authenticated the user.

The network access device (e.g. an Access Point), at this domain name, will then
complete the final phase of user authentication.

It is recommended to only set this override when using a custom server certificate
on the network access device. This is so that, when the user's web browser is
redirected back to the network access device, the host matches one of the valid
domain names in the certificate (e.g. a subject-alt-name, or the common name).

If set, this value must be a valid domain name.
network-login-host-override.x-typeDescription: A domain name (as supported by Cloud Guest) according to RFC 952 and RFC 1123.

register-accept-email.description:
Allow users to register using an email address.

The register-accept-email field can only be configured when enable-registration is true.

At least one of register-accept-email and register-accept-phone must be true when enable-registration is true.

register-accept-phone.description:
Allow users to register using a phone number.

The register-accept-phone field can only be configured when enable-registration is true.

At least one of register-accept-email and register-accept-phone must be true when enable-registration is true.

register-auto-expire.description:
Automatically set a registered account to expire after the specified duration (ISO 8601 duration).

The register-auto-expire field can only be configured when enable-registration is true.

registration-identity-store.description:
Reference to the identity store to be used for user registrations.

The registration-identity-store must be configured when enable-registration is true.

The registration-identity-store must be a member of the authentication profile's list of identity-stores when enable-registration is true.

The registration-identity-store must be a LOCAL identity store when enable-registration is true.

require-sponsor-approval.description:
Require sponsor approval for self-registrations.

If enabled, when a user is registering an account, they must provide the email address of a valid sponsor.
The sponsor will receive an email with a link to then review and approve the registration request.

The require-sponsor-approval field can only be configured when enable-registration is true.

At least one sponsor domain (see sponsor-domains), or sponsor email (see sponsor-emails), must be configured when require-sponsor-approval is true.

verify-registration.description:
Verify that the user actually owns the registered identity (e.g. email address or phone number) by sending an access code that must then be entered to complete registration.

The verify-registration field can only be configured when enable-registration is true.

items.description:
Allowed domain names for sponsor email addresses.

The maximum number of sponsor-domains allowed is 50.

When a user is registering an account that requires sponsor approval (see require-sponsor-approval), they must provide the email address of a valid sponsor.

Anyone who has an email address in one of these domains is a valid sponsor.

The sponsor-domains list can only be configured when require-sponsor-approval is true.

At least one sponsor domain (see sponsor-domains), or sponsor email (see sponsor-emails), must be configured when require-sponsor-approval is true.

See also the sponsor-emails field for configuring specific email addresses as valid sponsors.

Both the sponsor-domains and sponsor-emails lists define valid sponsors.

ArubaCdaPortalCustomMessage_CustomMessag.description:
A list of custom messages that can be used with Central NAC portal profiles
and visitor management facilities to provide customized email and SMS messages
for Central NAC visitors.

custom-message.description:
A custom message can be used with Central NAC portal profiles and visitor
management facilities to provide customized email and SMS messages for
Central NAC visitors.

description.description: A description, or note, for the custom message.

message-id.description: Unique identifier for the custom message (must be a UUID).

message-purpose.description:
The purpose of the custom message (e.g. PASSWORD_NOTICE, REGISTRATION, etc.).

The purpose defines where and how a custom message can be used, as well as
which replacement tokens must be included in the message.

The message types supported for each purpose are:

- PASSWORD_NOTICE: EMAIL, SMS
- REGISTRATION: EMAIL, SMS
- SPONSOR_APPROVAL: EMAIL only
- VERIFICATION_CODE: SMS only
- VERIFICATION_LINK: EMAIL only

The replacement tokens that must, or may, be included in the message content
for each purpose are:

- PASSWORD_NOTICE: may include: [account:username], [account:password]
- REGISTRATION: may include: [account:username], [account:password]
- SPONSOR_APPROVAL: must include: [account:sponsor-approval-url], may include: [account:username]
- VERIFICATION_CODE: must include: [account:verification-code]
- VERIFICATION_LINK: must include: [account:verification-url], may include: [account:username]

x-enumDescriptions.PASSWORD_NOTICE:
Specifies that the custom message can be used to notify the user about account
creation and password reset.

A password notice may contain the [account:username] and [account:password]
replacement tokens.
x-enumDescriptions.REGISTRATION:
Specifies that the custom message can be used to notify the user about
registration of their account.

A registration message may contain the [account:username] and
[account:password] replacement tokens.
x-enumDescriptions.SPONSOR_APPROVAL:
Specifies that the custom message can be used to notify the sponsor of a
registration request that requires their approval.

A sponsor approval message must contain the [account:sponsor-approval-url]
replacement token.

A sponsor approval message may contain the [account:username] replacement
token.

This purpose is only applicable to EMAIL messages.
x-enumDescriptions.VERIFICATION_CODE:
Specifies that the custom message can be used to send a verification code to
the user when registering an account.

A verification code message must contain the [account:verification-code]
replacement token.

This purpose is only applicable to SMS messages, as email messages use the
VERIFICATION_LINK purpose instead.
x-enumDescriptions.VERIFICATION_LINK:
Specifies that the custom message can be used to send a verification link to
the user when registering an account.

A verification link message must contain the [account:verification-url]
replacement token.

A verification link message may contain the [account:username] replacement
token.

This purpose is only applicable to EMAIL messages, as SMS messages use the
VERIFICATION_CODE purpose instead.

registration-email-message.description:
An optional custom email message to send on registration.

The message type of the referenced custom message must be EMAIL.

The message purpose of the referenced custom message must be REGISTRATION.

If not set, the default custom message of type EMAIL and purpose REGISTRATION
will be used. If no default custom message is defined, the system default
registration email message will be used.

registration-sms-message.description:
An optional custom SMS message to send on registration.

The message type of the referenced custom message must be SMS.

The message purpose of the referenced custom message must be REGISTRATION.

If not set, the default custom message of type SMS and purpose REGISTRATION
will be used. If no default custom message is defined, the system default
registration SMS message will be used.

require-accept-terms.description: Require the user to accept the terms and conditions of login page

sponsor-approval-email-message.description:
An optional custom email message to send when requesting sponsor approval.

The message type of the referenced custom message must be EMAIL.

The message purpose of the referenced custom message must be SPONSOR_APPROVAL.

If not set, the default custom message of type EMAIL and purpose
SPONSOR_APPROVAL will be used. If no default custom message is defined, the
system default sponsor approval email message will be used.

x-enumDescriptions.INTERNAL: Display terms and conditions inline on the sign-in page

ArubaCdaPortalSkinProfile_SkinsSchemaGET.description:
A list of portal skins that contain page-level layout and presentation
customizations to be applied to Central NAC portals (e.g. MPSK provisioning,
or captive portals).

skin.description:
A portal skin profile contains page-level layout and presentation
customizations to be applied to Central NAC portals (e.g. MPSK provisioning,
or captive portals).

background-color.description: The CSS color to apply to the background of the page
background-color.x-typeDescription:
A numerical CSS Color (Module Level 3).

Supported formats include:

#rgb             3 hex digit format

#rrggbb          6 hex digit format

rgb(r,g,b)       RGB functional notation with either 3 integers, or
                3 percentages

rgba(r,g,b,a)    RGBA functional notation with RGB values as per
                rgb(), and a 4th alpha-value

hsl(h,s,l):      HSL functional notation with H (hue) as either an
                integer (0 to 360) or a percentage, and
                S (saturation) and L (lightness) as percentages

hsla(h,s,l,a):   HSL functional notation with HSL values as per
                hsl(), and a 4th alpha-value

NOTE: alpha-values are only supported as floating point values,
        which should be in the range 0.0 (fully transparent) to 1.0
        (fully opaque).

The image to apply to the background of the page.

The UUID for this image must be a valid Central NAC image file identifier.

See the Central NAC image upload API for further details.

x-enumDescriptions.RIGHT: Align the brand (title and image) to the right of the page

rgb(r,g,b)       RGB functional notation with either 3 integers, or
                3 percentages

rgba(r,g,b,a)    RGBA functional notation with RGB values as per
                rgb(), and a 4th alpha-value

hsl(h,s,l):      HSL functional notation with H (hue) as either an
                integer (0 to 360) or a percentage, and
                S (saturation) and L (lightness) as percentages

hsla(h,s,l,a):   HSL functional notation with HSL values as per
                hsl(), and a 4th alpha-value

NOTE: alpha-values are only supported as floating point values,
        which should be in the range 0.0 (fully transparent) to 1.0
        (fully opaque).
